critical.mandate: Val is READ-ONLY on the target plan artifact — never modify the plan content itself, only append findings
WRITE-ONLY to the Plan Validation Findings section — findings go to the artifact as an appended section
Classify ALL findings by severity: CRITICAL, WARNING, INFO
Always verify claims against the filesystem — no trust, no assumptions
Frame findings constructively — suggestions, not accusations
Load validation-patterns skill sections JIT per step — never pre-load the full skill

step.title: Load and Parse Plan
step.action: Load the plan artifact specified by {plan_artifact_path}

Parse the plan document and extract: - All file targets with their action verbs (Create, Add, Modify, Update, Edit, Change, Fix, Delete, Remove) - All version bump statements (current → planned version strings) - All ADR references (e.g., ADR-012, ADR-xxx) - Implementation steps with their descriptions
Load validation-patterns:claim-extraction section JIT for structured claim parsing

Build a structured list of claims to verify: - file_targets: [{path, action_verb, plan_step}] - version_bumps: [{file, field, current_value, planned_value, plan_step}] - adr_references: [{adr_id, plan_step}]
step.check: Flag as WARNING: "Plan contains no concrete file targets — cannot verify implementation scope"

step.title: File Target Verification
step.action: Load validation-patterns:filesystem-verification section JIT

For each file target, classify by action verb and verify: - Create / Add / New / Generate → target file MUST NOT exist. If file exists: WARNING "File already exists but plan says Create — will overwrite" - Modify / Update / Edit / Change / Fix → target file MUST exist. If file missing: CRITICAL "File does not exist but plan step says Modify" - Delete / Remove → target file MUST exist. If file missing: WARNING "File does not exist — may have already been deleted"
For each file target, run filesystem check: existsSync or equivalent verification against {project-root}
Record each finding with: severity, description, plan step reference

step.title: Version Bump Verification
step.action: For each version bump statement found in the plan:

Parse semver strings (major.minor.patch) from both the plan text and the actual codebase files: - global.yaml: framework_version field - package.json: version field - Any other version-bearing files referenced in the plan

Compare planned version against actual current value: - Sequential bump (patch +1, minor +1 with patch reset, major +1 with minor/patch reset): No finding — valid - Non-sequential but valid (e.g., skip from 1.29.0 to 1.31.0): WARNING "Non-sequential version bump — skipping {skipped_version}" - Major version bump (e.g., 1.x → 2.x): INFO "Major version bump from {current} to {planned} — confirm intentional" - Planned version lower than or equal to current: CRITICAL "Planned version {planned} is not higher than current {current}"

step.title: Completeness Verification
step.action: Check if the plan covers related files that would typically need updating alongside the primary changes. These are heuristic checks at WARNING level, not hard requirements:

If plan modifies a workflow, check for mentions of: - workflow-manifest.csv - Slash command file (.claude/commands/) - checklist.md - lifecycle-sequence.yaml (if the workflow has sequencing implications)
If plan modifies an agent, check for mentions of: - agent-manifest.csv - Slash command file (.claude/commands/)
If plan modifies a skill, check for mentions of: - skill-registry (if applicable)
For each missing related file: WARNING "Plan does not mention updating {file}"

step.title: Architecture Cross-Reference
step.action: Check if ground truth exists at {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/ground-truth.md
step.check: INFO "Ground truth not available — cross-reference verification skipped". Skip remainder of this step.
step.action: If plan references ADRs (e.g., ADR-012), load the relevant ADR section from architecture.md

Verify planned changes align with ADR specifications: - Components specified in the ADR are addressed by the plan - Integration points mentioned in the ADR are covered - Constraints from the ADR are respected
For each misalignment: WARNING "Plan implements {ADR} but does not address: {component}"

step.title: Classify and Present Findings
step.action: Load validation-patterns:severity-classification section JIT
Compile all findings from Steps 2-5 into a single list, sorted by severity (CRITICAL first, then WARNING, then INFO)
step.check: Present: "Plan validation complete — no findings. Plan looks clean." Then skip to Step 7 (which will write a clean validation confirmation instead of a findings table).
step.action:
Present findings in a concise summary (one line per finding):
--- Validation Findings ({N}) --- | # | Severity | Finding | Plan Step | |---|----------|---------|-----------| | 1 | CRITICAL | File `path/to/file` does not exist but plan step says "Modify" | Step 3: Detailed Edits | | 2 | WARNING | Plan does not mention updating workflow-manifest.csv | — | | 3 | INFO | Major version bump 1.29.0 → 2.0.0 — confirm intentional | Step 4: Version Bumps |

Enter discussion loop: - Present each finding with its number - User can: [a]pprove finding, [d]ismiss finding, [e]dit finding - Continue until all findings are reviewed - Track which findings are approved vs dismissed
step.ask: Review the findings above. For each finding, indicate [a]pprove, [d]ismiss, or [e]dit. You can also approve/dismiss all at once.

step.title: Write Plan Validation Findings
step.action: Load validation-patterns:findings-formatting section JIT
Collect only the APPROVED findings (exclude dismissed ones)

Append the approved findings to the plan artifact under a new section:
## Plan Validation Findings
| # | Severity | Finding | Plan Step | |---|----------|---------|-----------| | 1 | CRITICAL | ... | ... | | 2 | WARNING | ... | ... |
**Validation date:** {date} **Validator:** Val **Total findings:** {approved_count} approved, {dismissed_count} dismissed

Append a clean validation confirmation:
## Plan Validation Findings
No findings — plan validated clean.
**Validation date:** {date} **Validator:** Val
step.template-output: Append Plan Validation Findings section with approved findings table, validation metadata, and summary counts.

step.title: Save to Val Memory
step.action:
Auto-save all validation results to Val's memory sidecar (no user prompt required):
1. Append to {project-root}/{memory_path}/validator-sidecar/decision-log.md using standardized format: ### [YYYY-MM-DD] Plan Validation: {plan_name}
- **Agent:** validator - **Workflow:** val-validate-plan - **Sprint:** {sprint_id or "N/A"} - **Status:** recorded - **Related:** {plan_artifact_path}
Validated plan: {plan_artifact_path}. File targets: {file_target_count}. Version bumps: {version_bump_count}. ADR refs: {adr_count}. Findings: {total_findings} ({approved_count} approved, {dismissed_count} dismissed). {Brief summary of key findings}
2. Replace body of {project-root}/{memory_path}/validator-sidecar/conversation-context.md (preserve header above first ---): Last session: Plan validation of {plan_name}. Date: {YYYY-MM-DD}. Findings: {total_findings}. Approved: {approved_count}. Dismissed: {dismissed_count}.
If {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/ directory or files don't exist, create them with standard headers: decision-log.md: "# Val Validator — Decision Log

> Chronological record of validation decisions.

---" conversation-context.md: "# Val Validator — Conversation Context

> Rolling summary of the most recent validation session.

---

No sessions recorded yet." If writing fails, log warning and continue — memory save is non-blocking.
